description: Create the ccb_navprove (Find and Prove) benchmark suite with shared verifier template, run script, task scaffolding, and registry integration. Agent writes a regression test that fails on buggy code and passes after applying a reference patch. Majority-of-3 voting for flakiness mitigation.

userStories.title: Create ccb_navprove suite directory and CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the ccb_navprove suite directory created with a CLAUDE.md that provides navigation-specific search strategy guidance for MCP tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory benchmarks/ccb_navprove/ exists

benchmarks/ccb_navprove/CLAUDE.md exists and contains: suite description mentioning 'Find and Prove', search strategy section listing MCP tools (keyword_search, find_references, go_to_definition, commit_search, diff_search, read_file), output requirements specifying regression test path /workspace/regression_test.{ext}, and constraints section (do NOT fix the bug, test timeout 60s)
CLAUDE.md follows the pattern of existing suite CLAUDE.md files (e.g., ccb_investigation/CLAUDE.md, ccb_nlqa/CLAUDE.md)
grep -c 'keyword_search\|find_references\|go_to_definition' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/CLAUDE.md returns at least 3
grep -c 'regression_test' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/CLAUDE.md returns at least 1
userStories.notes: Follow pattern from benchmarks/ccb_investigation/CLAUDE.md but adapted for bug investigation + test writing tasks.

userStories.title: Create shared find_and_prove_verifier.sh template
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want a reusable verifier shell script template that implements the 2-phase Find and Prove verification with majority-of-3 voting.
acceptanceCriteria: File benchmarks/ccb_navprove/_shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)

Script uses environment variables: AGENT_TEST_PATH (default /workspace/regression_test.py), REFERENCE_PATCH (default /tests/reference_fix.patch), TEST_COMMAND (default 'python3 -m pytest'), PATCH_APPLY_DIR (default /workspace)
Phase 1: runs TEST_COMMAND on AGENT_TEST_PATH 3 times, counts failures. Phase passes (0.5 score) if >=2 of 3 runs fail (non-zero exit)
Phase 2: applies REFERENCE_PATCH via git apply in PATCH_APPLY_DIR, runs TEST_COMMAND 3 times, counts passes. Phase passes (0.5 score) if >=2 of 3 runs pass (zero exit)
Edge cases: if AGENT_TEST_PATH does not exist or is empty, output 0.0 and exit. If REFERENCE_PATCH does not exist, score only phase 1 (max 0.5)
Each test run has a 60-second timeout (use 'timeout 60' command wrapper)
Logs all output to /logs/verifier/ directory (creates it if needed): phase1.log, phase2.log, summary.log
Final score (float 0.0-1.0) written to stdout as last line AND to /logs/verifier/reward.txt
bash -n benchmarks/ccb_navprove/_shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh passes (valid syntax)
shellcheck benchmarks/ccb_navprove/_shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh has no errors (warnings OK)
userStories.notes: This is the core reusable component. Each task's test.sh will source this and set the 4 env vars. Majority-of-3 is navprove-specific (other suites use single-shot). Test timeout is per-run (60s), not per-phase.

userStories.title: Create navprove_2config.sh run script
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want a run script that executes navprove tasks in paired baseline + SG_full configuration following the existing 2config pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: File configs/navprove_2config.sh exists and is executable
Script sources configs/_common.sh for shared infra
Script loads ~/evals/.env.local credentials
Script calls enforce_subscription_mode and ensure_fresh_token
TASKS_DIR points to benchmarks/ccb_navprove

ALL_TASK_IDS array contains 9 task IDs: navprove-qb-url-001, navprove-qb-tab-001, navprove-qb-bookmark-001, navprove-qb-download-001, navprove-ansible-vault-001, navprove-teleport-ssh-001, navprove-vuls-oval-001, navprove-flipt-cache-001, navprove-tutanota-search-001

TASK_SG_REPO_NAMES associative array maps all 9 tasks to their Sourcegraph repo names: qutebrowser tasks to github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser, ansible to github.com/ansible/ansible, teleport to github.com/gravitational/teleport, vuls to github.com/future-architect/vuls, flipt to github.com/flipt-io/flipt, tutanota to github.com/tutao/tutanota
Uses run_paired_configs for paired baseline + SG_full execution
Supports --parallel, --baseline-only, --full-only, --task flags (follows investigation_2config.sh pattern)
JOBS_BASE uses pattern runs/${CATEGORY}/navprove_${MODEL_SHORT}_${TIMESTAMP}
bash -n configs/navprove_2config.sh passes (valid syntax)
userStories.notes: Follow configs/investigation_2config.sh as template. Key differences: different TASKS_DIR, different task IDs, different SG repo mappings. Use --path mode (tasks are custom, not in harbor registry).

userStories.title: Add navprove to aggregate_status.py and generate_manifest.py
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the navprove suite recognized by the status scanner and MANIFEST generator so runs appear in reports.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/aggregate_status.py DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE dict contains entry: 'navprove_': 'ccb_navprove'
scripts/generate_manifest.py DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE dict contains entry: 'navprove_': 'ccb_navprove'
python3 -c "exec(open('scripts/aggregate_status.py').read().split('def ')[0]); print(DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE.get('navprove_'))" prints ccb_navprove
python3 -c "exec(open('scripts/generate_manifest.py').read().split('def ')[0]); print(DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE.get('navprove_'))" prints ccb_navprove
userStories.notes: Simple dict addition in both files. Add alphabetically near 'nlqa_' entry.

userStories.title: Create 5 Python task scaffolding (directories, task.toml, test.sh)
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the directory structure and boilerplate files for 5 Python Find and Prove tasks so that only the instruction.md, Dockerfile, and reference_fix.patch need manual creation.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 directories exist: benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-qb-url-001/, navprove-qb-tab-001/, navprove-qb-bookmark-001/, navprove-qb-download-001/, navprove-ansible-vault-001/
Each has task.toml with: name (task ID), difficulty='hard', time_limit_sec=1800, language='python'

Each has tests/test.sh that: sets AGENT_TEST_PATH=/workspace/regression_test.py, sets TEST_COMMAND='python3 -m pytest --timeout=60', sets REFERENCE_PATCH=/tests/reference_fix.patch, sets PATCH_APPLY_DIR=/workspace, then sources /tests/_shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh
Each has a placeholder instruction.md with TODO marker: '<!-- TODO: Write symptom-only bug description. Do NOT leak file paths, function names, or fix strategies. -->'
Each has a placeholder environment/Dockerfile with TODO marker: '# TODO: Set up buggy codebase at correct commit with test dependencies'
Each has an empty tests/reference_fix.patch placeholder with TODO marker: '# TODO: Add the known-good patch (git diff format)'
All test.sh files are executable (chmod +x) and pass bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: qutebrowser tasks: 7c956b62 (URL), a5bb165f (tab), d3e3076d (bookmark), f61e449f (download). ansible: b2a289dc (vault). The test.sh sources the shared verifier — the _shared dir is uploaded alongside each task's tests/ by Harbor at runtime, so the path should be /tests/_shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh. IMPORTANT: also copy the _shared directory into each task's tests/ dir so Harbor uploads it. Actually, Harbor uploads the specific task's tests/ dir only. So each test.sh must either inline the verifier or we need a different approach. Best approach: copy _shared/find_and_prove_verifier.sh into each task's tests/ as tests/find_and_prove_verifier.sh, then source ./find_and_prove_verifier.sh (relative to /tests/).

userStories.title: Create 3 Go task scaffolding
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the directory structure and boilerplate files for 3 Go Find and Prove tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 directories exist: benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-teleport-ssh-001/, navprove-vuls-oval-001/, navprove-flipt-cache-001/
Each has task.toml with: name (task ID), difficulty='hard', time_limit_sec=1800, language='go'

Each has tests/test.sh that: sets AGENT_TEST_PATH=/workspace/regression_test.go, sets TEST_COMMAND='go test -run TestRegression -v -timeout 60s', sets REFERENCE_PATCH=/tests/reference_fix.patch, sets PATCH_APPLY_DIR=/workspace, then sources the verifier
Each has a placeholder instruction.md, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/reference_fix.patch with TODO markers (same pattern as US-005)
Each tests/ directory contains a copy of find_and_prove_verifier.sh from _shared/
All test.sh files are executable and pass bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: teleport: 0415e422, vuls: 139f3a81, flipt: from SWE-Pro. Go test command uses -run TestRegression to match agent's test function name convention.

tests/ directory contains a copy of find_and_prove_verifier.sh
test.sh is executable and passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: Register 9 navprove tasks in selected_benchmark_tasks.json
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want all 9 navprove tasks registered in the task selection system with complete metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json contains 9 entries with benchmark='ccb_navprove'
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json')); print(len([t for t in d['tasks'] if t['benchmark']=='ccb_navprove']))" prints 9

All 9 entries have: task_name, benchmark='ccb_navprove', sdlc_phase='Debugging', category='navigation_verified', difficulty='hard', language (python/go/typescript as appropriate), repo (full GitHub org/repo format)
All 9 entries have mcp_benefit_score (float) and mcp_breakdown with cc, cfd, ssp, tcw components
Repos: qutebrowser/qutebrowser (4 tasks), ansible/ansible (1), gravitational/teleport (1), future-architect/vuls (1), flipt-io/flipt (1), tutao/tutanota (1)
json.load(open('configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json')) succeeds without error (valid JSON)
userStories.notes: MCP benefit scores: qutebrowser tasks should be high (large Python codebase, multi-file bugs). Use formula cc(0.25)+cfd(0.30)+ssp(0.20)+tcw(0.25). tcw=0.8 for navprove (heavy search needed). Estimate cc/cfd/ssp per-repo based on codebase size and file count.
